name: MCP23Sxx I/O Expander
description: The MCP23S08 component ([datasheet](http://ww1.microchip.com/downloads/en/DeviceDoc/MCP23008-MCP23S08-Data-Sheet-20001919F.pdf), [Digi-Key](https://www.digikey.com/product-detail/en/microchip-technology/MCP23S08-E-P/MCP23S08-E-P-ND/735954)) has 8 GPIOs that can be configured independently.

config_entries.label: ID
config_entries.description: The id to use for this MCP23S08 component.

config_entries.label: CS Pin
config_entries.description: The SPI chip select pin to use

config_entries.label: Mode
config_entries.description: Pin mode flags (input / output / pullup / pulldown / open_drain). Combine flags as needed — e.g. input + pullup for a button pulled to VCC.

config_entries.label: Open Drain
config_entries.description: Set the open drain mode flag.

config_entries.label: Inverted
config_entries.description: Invert the logical level. ``true`` swaps high/low in software so an active-low button reads as active when grounded.

config_entries.label: Deviceaddress
config_entries.description: The address of the chip. Defaults to `0`.

config_entries.label: Interrupt Pin
config_entries.description: The pin connected to the INT output of the MCP23S08. When configured, the component becomes interrupt-driven instead of polling — it only reads the chip when a pin actually changes state, significantly reducing SPI bus traffic and CPU usage. Must be an internal GPIO pin (not another expander pin).

config_entries.label: Open Drain Interrupt
config_entries.description: Configure interrupt pins to open-drain mode. Useful when the MCP23S08's power supply is greater than 3.3 volts. Note that these pins will require pull-up resistors (to 3.3 volts) when this mode is enabled.
